metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the live-preview endpoint contract for AC11: protocol (e.g. existing MCP tool, new REST endpoint), request payload (criterion id + sample text or fixture id), response shape, timeout, and which fixture set is used (`.rebar/evals/` or inline user text).
findings.finding: AC11's 'live must-fire/must-not-fire preview' in the visual editor is under-specified: the endpoint contract (protocol, request/response shape, timeout, fixture sourcing) is absent, and the plan acknowledges no such endpoint exists today — leaving the agent to design a net-new API surface without guidance.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC11: 'The visual editor authors a project criterion… and runs a live must-fire/must-not-fire preview.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the descriptor-resolution fallback for prompt-less DET project invariants: either (a) require a minimal `.rebar/prompts/project.<name>.md` stub (and document it in the how-to AC), or (b) define a synthetic descriptor built from the detector YAML's `description`/`id` fields when `get_prompt` returns `PromptNotFound` for a DET-tier criterion.
findings.finding: The descriptor-build path for DET-only (prompt-less) project invariants is undefined: `_descriptor_from_prompt` unconditionally calls `get_prompt(f'plan-review-{cid}', ...)` and raises `PromptNotFound` if no `.md` file exists. The plan introduces 'exec-tier-polymorphic (prompt-less DET vs LLM) criteria' and says `effective_criteria` resolves each id 'via `get_prompt(id, repo_root)` for its rubric' — but a DET project invariant has only a detector YAML, no prompt file. No fallback resolution path is specified for this case.
findings.location: § What exists vs. what we build — 'A shared descriptor abstraction: ... exec-tier-polymorphic (prompt-less DET vs LLM) criteria'; § Overlay schema — 'each resolved via `get_prompt(id, repo_root)` for its rubric'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `exec` vocabulary value for DET-tier criteria in the overlay schema (e.g. add `'DET'` as an explicit allowed value alongside `'1-TURN'`/`'2-STEP'`/`'AGENT'`) and document how the descriptor-builder branches on it.
findings.finding: The `exec` value vocabulary for the new DET tier is never defined in the overlay schema. The existing `criteria_routing.json` schema uses `exec` values `'1-TURN'`, `'2-STEP'`, `'AGENT'` (verified in registry.py:158, criteria_routing.json). The plan introduces a DET exec tier for project invariants but never names what string value the `exec` key must take (e.g. `'DET'`?), leaving implementers unable to write a conforming overlay entry or the descriptor builder unable to branch on it.
findings.location: § Overlay schema — 'per-criterion routing reuses existing keys: ... {exec, applies_at, block_threshold, default_posture, facet, checklist}'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"exec": "DET"` to the overlay schema's per-criterion routing vocabulary, and add a `DET` branch to `exec_tier()` in `registry.py` that skips the `get_prompt` call and instead uses the detector YAML's metadata to populate the descriptor.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the live-preview endpoint's request schema (minimum: criterion id + fixture text, or a full eval-case dict), response schema (e.g. `{ok, findings: [...], error?: string, skipped?: bool}`), and the fallback behavior when the LLM call errors or times out (analogous to the `/validate` endpoint's `unavailable:true` pattern already in the editor).
findings.finding: The editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview endpoint is listed as net-new ('no prompt-execution endpoint exists today') in AC11 and the 'What exists vs. what we build' section, but the plan never specifies: (a) the request schema (what inputs does the preview call take — a criterion id? a plan text fixture? a diff fixture?), (b) the response schema (what shape does the preview return — a P1Finding list? a pass/fail?), or (c) the fallback when the LLM call errors or times out mid-preview.
findings.location: § What exists vs. what we build — 'Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview (no prompt-execution endpoint exists today)'; AC: 'The visual editor authors a project criterion (prompt + routing form) and runs a live must-fire/must-not-fire preview.'
findings.suggested_fix: Model the preview endpoint on the existing `/validate` fallback pattern (editor.py:729-757): POST `{id, fixture_text, kind}` → 200 `{ok, findings: [...]}` on success, or 200 `{ok: false, unavailable: true, errors: [...]}` on LLM error/timeout — keeping it HTTP-200 always so the front-end maps the result uniformly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the `registry_version` code change itself (e.g., a `--legacy-regver` escape flag, a migration step to re-attest existing certs, or a documented binary-downgrade procedure) — acknowledgement that old certs go stale is insufficient per T4.
findings.finding: The `registry_version` code change that treats old attestations as stale is acknowledged ('stale → re-review, never a crash') but no rollback/back-out path for the CODE change itself is specified. Removing the overlay does not revert the new hash computation; once the binary is deployed, pre-upgrade attestations are permanently invalidated and there is no stated mechanism to recover without a binary downgrade.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a migration bullet: 'On first run after upgrade, a one-time re-attest command (`rebar attest --migrate`) re-signs existing valid attestations under the new regver basis, allowing projects to opt into the change without immediate staleness churn; binary downgrade restores old behavior.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a time-boxed spike for the editor live-preview endpoint (step 4) to validate latency profile, streaming vs. polling model, and integration with the existing MCP/server layer — given no such endpoint exists today, commit to the interaction architecture before full build-out.
findings.finding: The plan identifies 'no prompt-execution endpoint exists today' for the editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview (build step 4), but includes no spike or prototype to validate the latency, streaming, or architectural feasibility of this net-new server-side capability before committing to the full editor integration.
findings.location: Build order — step 4: 'Editor live-preview + authoring extension'; AC line 'The visual editor authors a project criterion (prompt + routing form) and runs a live must-fire/must-not-fire preview.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the caching contract for `effective_criteria(repo_root)` — either (a) it is uncached (call-through to `effective_routing` which is mtime-keyed), or (b) it is keyed by `(repo_root, overlay_mtime)` mirroring `effective_routing`, or (c) it must always be called through `_invalidate_caches()` — and confirm this contract applies to the descriptor-merge layer, not just the raw routing-index layer.
findings.finding: `effective_criteria(repo_root)` cache key is under-specified, risking cross-repo criterion-descriptor leakage in the long-lived MCP server.
findings.location: § Overlay schema, activation & caching — 'Caching (clears G6 cache bug)' paragraph; AC3 'Overlay merge is cache-isolated per repo'
findings.suggested_fix: Make `effective_criteria(repo_root)` uncached (or key it by `(repo_root, overlay_mtime)`), and have `_invalidate_caches()` also clear any descriptor-level cache. The simplest and most leakage-proof option: make `effective_criteria(repo_root)` a plain (uncached) function that calls through to the mtime-keyed `effective_routing`, keeping only the packaged `_routing_index` lru_cache'd. Extend `_invalidate_caches()` to clear any descriptor cache that does get added.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `effective_routing` caching strategy ('uncached' vs 'keyed by `(repo_root, overlay mtime)`') and specify the corresponding `_invalidate_caches()` extension: if uncached, remove the instruction to extend it; if mtime-keyed, name the cache data structure and the clear call.
findings.finding: The `_invalidate_caches()` extension for the new `effective_routing(repo_root)` overlay cache is specified in terms of outcome ('extend `prompt_library._invalidate_caches()`') but the placement and mechanism are ambiguous: `effective_routing` is described as 'uncached, or keyed by `(repo_root, overlay mtime)`' — if it is uncached, there is nothing to invalidate; if it is mtime-keyed, it is a different cache than the `@lru_cache` that `_invalidate_caches()` currently clears. The plan never resolves which variant is chosen, leaving the state-increment and its placement undefined.
findings.location: § Overlay schema — 'Caching (clears G6 cache bug): keep `_routing_index()` `@lru_cache`d ... add `effective_routing(repo_root)` (uncached, or keyed by `(repo_root, overlay mtime)`) ... extend `prompt_library._invalidate_caches()`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Thread `repo_root` through ALL three `registry_version()` call sites in `attest.py`: not just `compute_validity` but also `drift_refresh_candidate` (~line 279) and `remediation_mode_candidate` (~line 364), so overlay changes invalidate the drift-refresh candidate check and the remediation-mode check in addition to the direct claim gate.
findings.finding: The plan's '`registry_version(repo_root)` threading' claim is incomplete: `drift_refresh_candidate` and `remediation_mode_candidate` in `attest.py` both call `registry_version()` without threading `repo_root`, so overlay changes on those paths would not invalidate a prior attestation.
findings.location: § Attestation & invalidation — '`registry_version(repo_root=None)` additionally hashes the `.rebar/` overlay…thread `repo_root` through `claim_gate_check → compute_validity → registry_version`'; AC10
findings.suggested_fix: In `drift_refresh_candidate` and `remediation_mode_candidate`, pass the function's own `repo_root` parameter into `registry_version(repo_root=repo_root)`. Both functions already accept `repo_root=None` — this is a one-line fix per call site, but the plan must name it as a required change.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Formalize the 5 build-order steps as child tickets (or explicitly list which ACs belong to each step), and isolate the b744-blocked Step 5 as a separate dependent child so earlier steps can ship independently.
findings.finding: The epic has 13 ACs spanning at least 5 independent subsystems (registry+caching, attestation, DET-scan consumer, eval/calibration tooling, editor live-preview) plus a documentation deliverable, but the 5 build-order steps are described only as inline prose — no formal child ticket IDs or separate story boundaries are declared. This makes it hard to independently track, review, or hand off individual slices, and Step 5 is explicitly blocked on a separate epic (b744).
findings.location: Build order (risk-ordered MVP; clears G5 thin-slice) + Acceptance Criteria (13 items)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Child tickets' section mapping each build step to a ticket ID and its owned ACs, e.g.: Step 1 (MVP slice): ACs 1–3, 9–10; Step 2 (DET consumer): AC 5; Step 3 (eval runner): AC 8; Step 4 (editor): AC 11; Step 5 (unified registry, blocked on b744): ACs 7, 12–13.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC7's in-session scope: either mark it explicitly as 'deferred until b744 lands' (with a stub deliverable defined for the current session) or split it into a dependent follow-on AC so the current epic's AC list is fully executable without b744.
findings.finding: AC7 is presented as a flat acceptance criterion but is explicitly conditional on `b744` landing (WS6 Gerrit voter still pending); an executing agent cannot complete or evaluate it in the current session and has no specified fallback scope (stub? skip? partial unification?).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC7: 'A shared `rebar.llm.criteria` layer… (depends on b744)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical deliverable for the current session, e.g.: 'AC7 (b744-gated): Define and document the `rebar.llm.criteria` interface contract (types + routing adapter shim with TODO stubs); the live wiring of code-review is a follow-on once b744 WS6 lands.' This gives the agent an observable, in-session artifact.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the '(AC8)' label in the Attestation section: either retarget it to AC9/AC10 (which actually cover cross-gate attestation), or rename it to a non-colliding internal tag to avoid collision with the formal AC numbering.
findings.finding: The Attestation section uses '(AC8)' as an internal tracking label for the cross-gate attestation behavior, but AC8 in the formal Acceptance Criteria list is the eval-runner CLI (`rebar criteria eval <id>`). The cross-gate attestation behavior described under that label is actually covered by ACs 9 and 10 — creating a misleading cross-reference that would send an implementer to the wrong requirement.
findings.location: Attestation & invalidation section (label '**Cross-gate (AC8):**') vs. Acceptance Criteria list, AC8
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '**Cross-gate (AC8):**' with '**Cross-gate (see AC9, AC10):**' in the Attestation section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a documentation/ADR deliverable to 'What exists vs. what we build (Net-new)' and insert a corresponding step (or explicit sub-task within step 5) in the Build order so AC13 has plan-body coverage.
findings.finding: AC13 (ADR + `CLAUDE.md`/`docs/` section documenting the unified registry contract, overlay merge, attestation-invalidation protocol, and authoring how-to) is an orphan acceptance criterion: it has no corresponding deliverable description in any plan-body section and no step in the Build order.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC13 (final item); Build order section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly include in AC13 (or the doc plan) an update to existing `criteria_routing.json` schema references to clearly distinguish the packaged (flat, per-gate) format from the `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` overlay (gate-keyed wrapper) format, preventing stale references from misleading project authors.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` overlay with a gate-keyed schema that wraps (but is distinct from) the packaged per-gate `criteria_routing.json` schema. Existing documentation and tooling that reference the packaged `criteria_routing.json` schema (flat, per-gate) are not called out for update, risking stale references that will confuse project authors trying to author their overlay.
findings.location: § Overlay schema, activation & caching; AC2; AC13
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC13: 'Update existing schema docs/references for the packaged `criteria_routing.json` to note that the `.rebar/` overlay uses a gate-keyed wrapper (`{"plan_review": {…}, "code_review": {…}, "activate": […]}`) distinct from the flat packaged format; cross-link from both.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the expand-contract / backward-compat guarantee explicitly to the `disabled_builtins` coverage-manifest field: document (a) how old readers encountering the new field behave (ignore unknown fields — confirm no strict-schema validator rejects it), and (b) how new readers encountering old certs without the field behave (treat as 'no builtins disabled' — confirm this is safe, not a crash).
findings.finding: The plan adds a new `disabled_builtins` field to the attestation coverage manifest (persisted in signed certs) but does not explicitly extend its expand-contract / backward-compat guarantee to cover this field, leaving ambiguity about whether existing cert consumers (e.g., `claim_gate_check`, any external tooling parsing the signed coverage section) will handle the new field gracefully on partial-upgrade or rollback.
findings.location: § Attestation & invalidation — 'Disable recorded in coverage: a disabled built-in appears in the manifest coverage (an explicit field)'; AC: 'a disable is recorded in attestation coverage'
findings.suggested_fix: In §Attestation, add a sentence parallel to the existing registry_version one: 'The `disabled_builtins` field is additive; an existing cert lacking it is treated as "no builtins disabled" (not a crash); readers MUST use `dict.get("disabled_builtins", [])`, not direct key access. Confirm no coverage-schema validator enforces presence of this field.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove the 'it can never rebind a built-in' clause from AC9 (or add a cross-reference to AC2) since AC2 already specifies the rebind-rejection rule with full load-time semantics.
findings.finding: The no-rebind rule is stated as a verifiable requirement in both AC2 and AC9, creating a near-duplicate that could lead to redundant test coverage or, worse, one site being weakened without updating the other.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC9, replace 'it can never rebind a built-in' with '(rebind rejected per AC2)'.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
